docs.title: PiHerder documentation
docs.text:
Secure fleet management for Raspberry Pi and Linux hosts — backups, patching, Docker control, and service templates with secrets encrypted at rest.

Latest release v0.4.0 In development v0.5.0 plan Source github.com/bjorngluck/piherder Docs (this site) bjorngluck.github.io/piherder License PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 (non-commercial source-available)

docs.title: Start here
docs.text:
• Install in ~15 minutes

    Docker Compose, master key, first admin user.

    Install guide

  • Add your first Pi

    SSH key deploy, least-priv user, feature flags.

    Add a server

  • Deploy a service template

    NPM, Uptime Kuma, Pi-hole, Grafana — wizard + secrets.

    Templates

  • Something broken?

    SSH, rsync, backups, push, template stacks.

    Troubleshooting

docs.title: What PiHerder does
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  You[Browser / PWA] --> Caddy[Caddy TLS]
  Caddy --> Web[FastAPI web]
  Web --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]
  Web --> Redis[(Redis)]
  Celery[Celery workers] --> DB
  Celery --> Redis
  Celery -->|SSH · rsync · docker · apt| Fleet[Pi / Linux fleet]
  Web -.->|deep links| Kuma[Uptime Kuma]
  Web -.->|deep links| GF[Grafana]
  • Fleet ops: rsync backups, apt OS patch, Docker compose projects
  • Safety: Fernet-encrypted SSH keys, audit trail, RBAC, optional 2FA + Web Push
  • Templates: versioned stacks with variables, desired state, step-up secrets
  • Integrations (optional): Uptime Kuma + Grafana bindings

Core fleet work never requires Kuma, Grafana, or templates.

docs.title: Documentation map
docs.text:
Section For Getting started First install & HTTPS Day to day Servers, backups, updates, jobs Docker Host containers & compose Templates Deploy / from-host / secrets Integrations Kuma & Grafana Account & security RBAC, users, 2FA, PWA Operations Env, DR, metrics, API Developers Code, tests, schema, publish

Maintainer roadmaps and release plans stay in the repo under docs/ — not in this user-facing tree.

docs.title: Screenshots
docs.text: Dashboard — fleet summary and attention table. wireframe Replace with a real capture when ready (see wiki/assets/screenshots/README.md).

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• Interactive API (on your instance): /docs (OpenAPI, tag api-v1)
  • Security policy for the project: SECURITY.md
  • Report issues: GitHub Issues

docs.title: PWA & Web Push
docs.text: Enable device + event toggles. wireframe

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Trusted HTTPS + stable PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL.
  2. Web service running (VAPID auto-generated on startup).

docs.title: VAPID (default)
docs.text:
On web startup PiHerder auto-generates a VAPID key pair once and stores it in Postgres (pushvapidconfig). Private key is Fernet-encrypted with PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY. No script required for normal use.

Logs: Web Push VAPID ready (source=generated) (or source=env if overriding).

Do not rotate keys casually

Changing the VAPID private key invalidates every device subscription; users must re-enable push.

docs.title: Optional env pin
docs.text:
# Only if you intentionally pin keys after DB wipe
# VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY=...
# VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY=...
# VAPID_CONTACT=mailto:admin@yourdomain.com

docs.title: Android (Chrome)
docs.text:
1. Install PWA if prompted.
  2. Account → Push notifications → Enable on this device.
  3. Send test notification (your devices only).
  4. Toggle event types and save.

docs.title: iPhone / iPad (iOS 16.4+)
docs.text:
1. Safari → Share → Add to Home Screen.
  2. Open the Home Screen icon (not a plain Safari tab).
  3. Account → Enable on this device.

Push does not work from a plain Safari tab.

docs.title: When push fires
docs.text: Only when a new open in-app notification is created (not on every fingerprint refresh).

docs.title: In-app notifications without push
docs.text: The bell inbox still works if VAPID is unavailable.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Push / PWA

docs.title: Roles (RBAC)
docs.text:
Three roles, lowest → highest privilege:

Role Read fleet UI Run backups / patch / Docker Manage users viewer Yes No (mutating HTTP blocked except self-service) No operator Yes Yes No admin Yes Yes Yes (/auth/users)

docs.title: Viewer self-service (allowed writes)
docs.text:
• Log out
  • Edit account (profile, password, avatar)
  • Manage own 2FA
  • First-login password change / force-2FA onboarding
  • Dismiss notifications
  • Own Web Push subscription + prefs

Viewers cannot start jobs, change servers, open Users, or change security policy.

docs.title: Enforcement
docs.text:
• Logged-in roles can GET most pages (read browsing).
  • Mutating methods checked in auth middleware.
  • User admin routes always require admin.
  • Missing legacy role → treated as admin.

docs.title: Sole admin protection
docs.text: You cannot demote or delete the last active admin. Promote another user first.

docs.title: Optional per-user 2FA
docs.text: Account → enable TOTP, save backup codes, optional trusted device (30 days, revocable).

docs.title: Force 2FA for all
docs.text:
Where: Settings → Security policy.

Setting Effect Force 2FA for all Users without TOTP go to /auth/force-2fa before the fleet UI. Password change-on-first-login still runs first if required.

Stored in PostgreSQL (appsetting) — travels with DB dumps and self-backup.

docs.title: Template step-up
docs.text:
Viewing cleartext template secrets requires a separate TOTP unlock (step-up), even if you already completed login 2FA. See Template secrets.

Optional: Require 2FA for template deploy & secrets in Security policy.

docs.title: Users
docs.text:
Where: avatar menu → Users (admin only) · /auth/users

Each card shows last login (app timezone) and a link to that user’s Audit trail.

docs.title: Create a user
docs.text:
1. Enter email and role (viewer / operator / admin).
  2. Generate password (or set manually). Strength meter + policy apply.
  3. One-time panel: login URL, email, temporary password, invite text — shown once.
  4. New users have must_change_password until first reset.

docs.title: Password policy
docs.text:
• ≥ 10 characters
  • Upper + lower + digit
  • Max 72 bytes

docs.title: Roles and delete
docs.text:
• Change role from the list (sole-admin rules).
  • Delete requires confirm; you cannot delete yourself.

docs.title: Open registration
docs.text: Only the first account self-registers. After that, only admins create users.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Servers → Add server (or equivalent CTA).
  2. Enter name, hostname/IP, SSH port/user.
  3. Generate a keypair (recommended) or upload a private key.
  4. Optionally store a one-time SSH password only to bootstrap key deploy.
  5. Save, open the server → SSH access.
SSH access: deploy key, test, rotate, least-priv, dependency chips. wireframe

docs.title: SSH access panel
docs.text:
Action What it does Test connection Verifies key (or password) login Deploy key Installs public key into authorized_keys; verifies key-only login Rotate key New keypair, deploy, swap only after verify succeeds Least-priv user Optional piherder user + limited sudoers (Pi OS / Ubuntu) Re-check dependencies Probes rsync / docker / apt for enabled features

Clear stored passwords

After key auth works, clear any stored SSH password so secrets stay keys-only (encrypted at rest with PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY).

docs.title: Least-privilege user (Debian / Pi OS / Ubuntu)
docs.text:
• Creates e.g. piherder with key-only login
  • Optional docker group
  • Sudoers for rsync/test and optional apt/reboot (visudo -cf before install)
  • Run on host re-points ssh_username after verify
  • HAOS / specialised: instructions only — not automated

docs.title: Docker base dir (Option B)
docs.text:
If stacks live under another user’s home (e.g. /home/bjorn/docker):

  1. Set Docker base dir to that absolute path (not ~/docker after switching to the piherder user).
  2. Run the Option B ACL script from SSH access so the service user can traverse the tree.

~/docker expands to the SSH user’s home and breaks restart/build/logs after re-pointing to piherder.

docs.title: Feature flags
docs.text:
Edit → Features — enable only what you need:

Flag Unlocks Backups rsync backup/restore UI + schedules OS patch apt check/apply Docker / containers Docker page, container patch, templates deploy targets

Disabled features are hard-hidden from dest cards and ⋯ menus.

docs.title: Schedules
docs.text: Edit → Schedules — update checks (safe) and optional apply (real upgrades). See Updates & patching.

docs.title: Host dependency check
docs.text:
After key deploy / least-priv / test, PiHerder stores a dependency snapshot. Failures show install/privilege hints only — nothing is auto-installed on the remote.

Server detail with status chips and feature cards. wireframe

docs.title: Backups & restore
docs.text: Per-server rsync over SSH to the PiHerder backup volume. Runs on Celery (not inside the web request).

docs.title: Enable backups
docs.text:
1. Server Edit → Features → enable Backups.
  2. Open the server’s Backups page.
  3. Add source paths on the remote host.
  4. Optional: destination override, retention, cron schedule.
  5. Path allow/deny — default deny of OS roots; optional prefixes.
Sources, policy, schedule, restore wizard. wireframe

docs.title: How success is decided
docs.text:
• Each source must finish with rc == 0 and no error classification.
  • Failed runs: status failed, audit error details, last_backup_at not updated.
  • Successful backups resolve open backup_failed notifications.

docs.title: rsync path
docs.text:
• Default: --rsync-path "sudo -n rsync" (or local sudo).
  • Root user / HAOS: plain rsync is auto-probed and used.

docs.title: Schedules
docs.text: Enable + cron on the Backups page. Same server never runs two backups at once (Redis mutex); different hosts can run in parallel.

docs.title: Restore wizard
docs.text:
1. Backups page → restore for a source.
  2. Dry-run reverse rsync (preview).
  3. Confirm to apply.
  4. Path policy enforced; audit action backup_restore.

Restore is privileged

You are writing back onto the remote host. Prefer dry-run first.

docs.title: Retention
docs.text: Retention cleanup is a separate job type (retention) driven by configured keep rules.

docs.title: Not the same as PiHerder self-backup
docs.text:
Server backups Herder self-backup Data Files from fleet hosts PiHerder DB config, users, keys… UI Server → Backups Settings → PiHerder backup Volume /backups /herder_backups

See Self-backup & DR.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Backups stuck or failing

docs.title: Jobs, audit & notifications
docs.text:
Three related systems — do not confuse them.

System Purpose Jobs Queue + live progress of work units Audit Immutable history (who / what / when / snippet) Notifications Dismissible inbox (updates pending, failed backup, …)

Fleet Jobs with filters and detail modal. wireframe

docs.title: Jobs
docs.text: Where: nav Jobs (/jobs) · compact panel on each server detail.

docs.title: Job types (examples)
docs.text:
Type Typical trigger backup Manual or backup cron → Celery os_patch / container_patch Manual or apply schedule os_update_check / container_update_check Manual or check schedule retention Retention cleanup herder_backup PiHerder self-backup

Statuses: pending → running → success / failed.

docs.title: Fleet Jobs UI
docs.text:
• Filters: server, status, type, date range, per-page
  • Active only — pending + running
  • Row → detail modal (summary, log tail, scheduled flag)
  • Cancel works from list and modal (where applicable)
  • Link to Audit for historical trail

docs.title: Live progress
docs.text: JobHold / progress modals poll status and log lines for OS/container patch and similar work.

docs.title: Audit
docs.text:
Actors may be:

  • Session user (display name + email)
  • API token name + id (automation)
  • system / scheduler for cron jobs

Filter by user, server, token, etc.

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text:
• Bell icon → open / dismiss
  • Deep links into the relevant server or page
  • Optional Web Push for new open notifications — PWA & Web Push
  • Dismiss is idempotent if already closed

docs.title: API
docs.text: Automation can list/trigger jobs with Bearer tokens — API tokens.

docs.title: Remove a server
docs.text: Where: Server detail → Remove from PiHerder (danger zone).

docs.title: What happens
docs.text:
Happens Does not happen Server row + stored SSH credentials removed from DB No SSH / remote changes by default Schedules unregistered; active jobs cancelled Docker stacks, volumes, media untouched Compose drafts in PiHerder deleted Host piherder user / sudoers / keys left as-is Jobs / audit / notifications unlinked (history kept) Backup archives on the backup volume kept

Confirm by typing the exact server name.

docs.title: Optional host cleanup
docs.text:
To drop the least-priv account on the host (as root):

  • Server detail → Copy script / Download .sh
  • Or SSH access → Host cleanup script
  • Repo: scripts/cleanup-piherder-user.sh
# On the host
sudo bash cleanup-piherder-user.sh                 # sudoers + docker group; keep user
USER_NAME=piherder REMOVE_USER=1 sudo -E bash cleanup-piherder-user.sh
DRY_RUN=1 sudo -E bash cleanup-piherder-user.sh    # preview

Does not remove Docker projects or data. Does not remove the server from the UI — do that separately if still listed.

docs.title: Updates & patching
docs.text: Two layers: check (safe, detect only) and apply (real upgrades). Silent auto-upgrade is never the default.

docs.title: Feature flags
docs.text: Feature Edit → Features OS packages OS patch Container images Docker / containers

docs.title: Update checks (safe)
docs.text:
Configured under Edit → Schedules.

Schedule Does Does not OS packages (apt) Count ready packages, phased count, reboot-pending Run upgrade Container images Pull/compare image IDs per compose project compose up -d

Results feed the dashboard, badges, and notifications.

docs.title: Patch apply (opt-in)
docs.text:
Off by default. Requires the matching feature flag.

Option Behaviour Enable scheduled apply Registers APScheduler job Only when last check found updates Skips if last check count is 0 OS: full-upgrade Uses full-upgrade instead of upgrade (+ update + autoremove) Cron e.g. weekly Sunday 30 3 * * 0

Also skipped when a job of the same type is already pending/running on that server.

Scheduled work is audited as system / scheduler.

docs.title: Manual apply
docs.text:
• Server list / detail: update / upgrade XOR full-upgrade / autoremove.
  • Live progress modal streams apt output.
  • Ubuntu phased packages counted separately (listed vs installable).
  • Container patch: compose pull + conditional up -d with live logs.

docs.title: Docker: Check updates vs Deploy
docs.text:
UI action Meaning Check updates Pull / compare only Deploy Pull + up -d — surfaces pull/up results (not silent success)

Successful Deploy clears pending stack badges and resolves container_updates when none remain.

docs.title: Reboot
docs.text: Least-priv sudoers may allow /usr/sbin/reboot. After a successful reboot command, PiHerder clears reboot_pending so the UI does not stick.

docs.title: Developers
docs.text:
Contributor-facing docs. Operator guides live in the rest of this site.

Topic Page Local setup setup Architecture architecture Template schema templates-schema Testing testing Publish image publish-image Contributing to this wiki contributing-docs

docs.title: Collaboration
docs.text: Public contributors may open Issues and Pull Requests. Only the project maintainer merges to main. See CONTRIBUTING.md.

docs.title: Repo maintainer docs (not in this nav)
docs.text: Path Role SPEC.md Canonical phase checklist docs/ROADMAP_ECOSYSTEM.md Multi-horizon roadmap docs/PLAN_v*.md Release plans docs/FEATURE_PLAN_*.md Feature designs docs/RELEASE_v*.md Release notes docs/DECISION_*.md Locked decisions

docs.title: Stack
docs.text: FastAPI · SQLModel · PostgreSQL · Paramiko · Fernet · Jinja2 · HTMX · Alpine · Tailwind (vendored) · APScheduler · Celery · Caddy

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
flowchart TB
    Browser["Browser (HTMX + Alpine)"] -->|HTTPS| Caddy
    Caddy --> FastAPI["FastAPI (web)"]

    subgraph Core["Docker Compose (supported)"]
        FastAPI --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]
        FastAPI --> Scheduler["APScheduler"]
        FastAPI --> Celery["Celery worker(s)"]
    end

    Scheduler -->|enqueue| Celery
    Celery -->|reads/writes| DB
    Celery -->|SSH + rsync/docker/apt| PiFleet["Remote fleet"]
    FastAPI -->|DB reads for UI| DB
    FastAPI -.->|Job.details progress| Celery

docs.title: Key modules (pointers)
docs.text: Concern Location Roles / middleware app/security/auth.py Password policy app/services/password_policy.py Jobs / progress app/services/jobs.py Scheduler app/services/scheduler.py Backup app/services/backup.py (+ progress, profiles) Docker inventory app/services/docker_inventory.py Templates app/services/service_templates/ Integrations app/services/integrations/ Push app/services/push.py API tokens app/services/api_tokens.py, app/routers/api_v1.py Herder backup app/services/herder_backup.py Metrics app/services/metrics.py

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text:
• Privileged actions audited
  • Secrets encrypted at rest; decrypt only in memory for jobs
  • Offline/air-gapped ready once built (vendored assets)
  • External/dangerous actions opt-in: preview → confirm → audit

docs.title: Contributing to this wiki
docs.text: Docs are Markdown in git under wiki/, built with MkDocs Material.

docs.title: Edit flow
docs.text:
1. Edit or add pages under wiki/.
  2. Register new pages in mkdocs.yml → nav:.
  3. Preview locally:
pip install -r requirements-docs.txt
mkdocs serve
  1. Open a PR / merge to main.

Live docs

Public site: https://bjorngluck.github.io/piherder/. After merge to main, rebuild and publish the site (mkdocs build → gh-pages, or the Docs GitHub Action if Pages uses Actions).

docs.title: Style
docs.text:
• Short pages, one job each (not another 750-line ADMIN).
  • Prefer numbered steps + tables + admonitions.
  • Code blocks for every command an operator must run.
  • Screenshots: real PNGs in wiki/assets/screenshots/ when available; wireframe SVGs are interim.
  • Do not put PLAN_* / FEATURE_PLAN_* / SPEC checklists in the user nav — link out to GitHub raw/blob if needed.

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
<figure class="ph-figure" markdown>
  ![Alt text](../assets/screenshots/dashboard.png)
  <figcaption>Describe what the operator should notice.</figcaption>
</figure>

Capture guide: wiki/assets/screenshots/README.md.

docs.title: Mermaid
docs.text: Fenced mermaid blocks render in Material (diagrams for architecture and flows).

docs.title: Build strictness
docs.text:
mkdocs build --strict

Fix warnings (broken links, missing files) before merge.

docs.title: Publish image
docs.text: Multi-arch images on Docker Hub / GHCR (target v0.5.0 RC). Full maintainer checklist: docs/PUBLISH_IMAGE.md.

docs.title: One-time Hub setup
docs.text:
1. Account on hub.docker.com
  2. Create repo bjorngluck/piherder (public)
  3. Access token (Read/Write) → docker login -u bjorngluck
  4. Link description to GitHub + these docs

docs.title: Tags
docs.text:
Tag Meaning 0.5.0 Immutable release 0.5 Latest patch in line (optional) latest Current stable

Images: bjorngluck/piherder or ghcr.io/bjorngluck/piherder.

docs.title: Multi-arch build example
docs.text:
export IMAGE=bjorngluck/piherder
export VERSION=0.5.0

docker buildx create --use --name piherder-builder 2>/dev/null || true
docker buildx build \
  --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 \
  -t "${IMAGE}:${VERSION}" \
  -t "${IMAGE}:latest" \
  --push \
  .

docs.title: Compose with a published image
docs.text:
web:
  image: bjorngluck/piherder:0.5.0
celery-worker:
  image: bjorngluck/piherder:0.5.0

docs.title: Until published
docs.text: docker compose up -d --build

docs.title: Docker Compose (recommended)
docs.text: Same as Install. Use Caddyfile.dev for self-signed TLS if needed.

docs.title: Python host (partial)
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"
# Need Postgres + Redis (compose up db redis) and .env
uvicorn app.main:app --reload

Schema: Alembic on startup, or:

alembic upgrade head

docs.title: Frontend assets
docs.text:
Build step vendors Tailwind/HTMX/Alpine. Offline image needs successful vendor at build time.

bash scripts/vendor_cdns.sh
# or VENDOR_INSECURE=1 if TLS intercept issues

docs.title: Docs site (this wiki)
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements-docs.txt
mkdocs serve
# open http://127.0.0.1:8000

See Contributing docs.

docs.title: Template schema
docs.text: Operator plan / schema detail also in docs/FEATURE_PLAN_TEMPLATES.md.

docs.title: template.yaml (sketch)
docs.text:
schema_version: 1
slug: uptime-kuma
name: Uptime Kuma
description: Monitoring with a simple UI
category: monitoring      # proxy | dns | monitoring | observability | other
version: "1.0.0"
tags: [kuma, monitoring]

variables:
  - name: PROJECT_NAME
    label: Project folder name
    type: string            # string | int | port | password | email | url | boolean | volume
    default: uptime-kuma
    required: true
    secret: false
  - name: ADMIN_PASSWORD
    type: password
    secret: true
    generate: true
  - name: FEATURE_X
    type: boolean
    default: "false"
  - name: APP_DATA
    type: volume
    volume_target: /app/data
    volume_default_mode: named
    default: app_data

files:
  - path: docker-compose.yml
  - path: .env
    from: .env.sample

checklist:
  - title: DNS (manual)
    body: |
      Create an A/AAAA record…

docs.title: Substitution
docs.text:
• Placeholders: {{VAR_NAME}} only (case-sensitive).
  • No arbitrary Jinja/code in untrusted imports.
  • Secrets → encrypted in PiHerder; written to host .env mode 600.

docs.title: Code
docs.text: Area Path Schema / catalog app/services/service_templates/ Router app/routers/templates_svc.py Disk starters service_templates/ Migration migrations/versions/…016_service_templates.py Tests tests/test_service_templates.py

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
# Inside compose (recommended)
docker compose run --rm --no-deps web pytest -q

# Host venv
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pytest -q

Unit tests live under tests/ — no live SSH required for the main suite.

Examples: test_rbac.py, test_api_tokens.py, test_service_templates.py, test_backup_paths.py, test_herder_backup.py, …

docs.title: Before a release
docs.text:
1. pytest -q green
  2. Manual smoke: register, add server, backup, template deploy, metrics, API token
  3. See release checklist in docs/RELEASE_v*.md / PLAN_v0.5.0

docs.title: Multi-file projects
docs.text:
On Docker → Edit compose, PiHerder loads when present:

  • docker-compose.yml (or compose.yaml)
  • override file
  • .env
  • Dockerfile

Tabs edit each file; Save & Deploy writes the full set and redeploys. Version history stores multi-file snapshots (merge-on-save so one file no longer wipes the others).

On the host, Compose still auto-loads override + .env in the project directory.

docs.title: Check updates vs Deploy
docs.text: Button Effect Check updates Pull / image compare — no up -d Deploy Pull + up -d; audit + banner show pull/up exit codes and output

docs.title: New project wizard
docs.text: Creates a project directory under the Docker base dir and optional initial compose.

docs.title: Template stacks
docs.text: If a project is template-managed, prefer the deployment / redeploy flow. Full compose edit is intentionally gated so desired state stays authoritative.

docs.title: Docker inventory cache
docs.text:
Compose/container lists are a DB snapshot, not a blocking full SSH inventory on every page open.

Behaviour Detail Storage Per-server docker_inventory_* columns Open Docker page Renders last snapshot immediately Refresh Background L1 collect (no expensive mount du on list path) Triggers Stale on open, after mutations, fleet job ~every 10 minutes, Force refresh Stale UI Banner “Inventory as of …” / “Refreshing…”; last good list kept Feature gate Only servers with Docker feature on

docs.title: Container expand
docs.text:
Full mount paths + per-path host du run on expand (GET …/docker/container/mounts). List stays fast.

Looks wrong after host-side changes?

Use Force refresh on the Docker page.

docs.title: Docker on hosts
docs.text:
When Docker / containers is enabled for a server, PiHerder can list compose projects, view logs, edit multi-file compose, build, and redeploy.

Docker dest card on server detail. wireframe

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Feature flag Docker / containers on.
  2. Remote docker usable by the SSH user (group/socket).
  3. Docker base dir set correctly (absolute path if using least-priv).
  4. Dependency check green for docker — SSH troubleshooting.

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text: Action Notes Browse projects / containers From inventory snapshot Logs Per container / service Multi-file compose edit compose + override + .env + Dockerfile Version history Snapshots; rollback Build / redeploy Wait for job feedback Check updates vs Deploy Pull-only vs pull+up — Updates New project wizard Create a stack on the host Template-managed stacks Badge + gated full editor — Templates

docs.title: Template vs free-form stacks
docs.text: Kind Edit path Template-managed Desired state / redeploy on deployment page; compose editor gated Free-form Full compose multi-file editor

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Inventory cache
  • Compose edit & deploy
  • Service templates

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: This section gets you from zero to a running PiHerder with a first admin account.

docs.title: Path
docs.text:
1. Requirements — what you need on the host and the fleet
  2. Install (Docker Compose) — clone, .env, docker compose up
  3. First login — register admin, lock registration
  4. Trusted HTTPS & TLS — required for PWA / mobile Web Push

Then continue with Add a server.

docs.title: Supported install path
docs.text:
Docker Compose only

The supported topology is the compose stack in this repository (web, db, redis, celery-worker, caddy).

Kubernetes and bare-metal installs are under consideration only — not documented as supported paths.

docs.title: Time estimate
docs.text: Step Typical Clone + generate secrets 5 min First compose up --build 5–15 min (image build) Register + open UI 2 min Trusted certs (if not ready) depends on your CA / ACME First server + key deploy 10–20 min

docs.title: Related repo docs
docs.text: Doc Role README Project overview .env.example Full env catalog docs/ADMIN.md Long-form admin reference (mirrored into this wiki)

docs.title: Register the first admin
docs.text:
1. Open the app URL from Install.
  2. Complete Register with a strong email + password.
  3. You are the first user → role admin.

Open registration locks after the first account

Further users are created only by an admin under Users — see Users.

docs.title: Password policy
docs.text:
Enforced on register, password change, and admin-created users:

  • At least 10 characters
  • At least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit
  • Max 72 bytes (bcrypt)

docs.title: After login checklist
docs.text: Step Where Set display name / avatar Account Optional 2FA Account → TOTP — or force 2FA for all Timezone Settings → General Create operators/viewers Users (admin) Add first server Add a server

docs.title: Admin quick checklist
docs.text:
1. Create operators/viewers; share one-time invite passwords carefully.
  2. Optionally enable Force 2FA under Settings → Security policy.
  3. Per server: Edit → Features → then Schedules for checks → only then consider apply schedules.
  4. Prefer “only if updates” on apply schedules; start with a quiet weekly window.
  5. For mobile push: trusted TLS + PWA & Web Push.
  6. DR: Settings → PiHerder backup; keep PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY offline safe.
After login you land near the fleet dashboard. wireframe

docs.title: Trusted HTTPS & TLS
docs.text: Android installable PWA and Web Push need a secure context with a trusted certificate and a stable origin. Self-signed Caddy (Caddyfile.dev / tls internal) is fine for desktop poking; it is not reliable for push on phones.

docs.title: Hostname and public URL
docs.text:
In .env (compose loads these for web and caddy):

PIHERDER_HOSTNAME=piherder.example.com
# Include :8443 when using compose host mapping 8443→443
PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL=https://piherder.example.com:8443
  • DNS: point PIHERDER_HOSTNAME at the host (or your outer reverse proxy).
  • Ports (default compose): HTTP 8888→80, HTTPS 8443→443.

docs.title: Volume-mounted certificates (recommended)
docs.text:
1. Place PEMs in certs/ (gitignored):
File Role certs/fullchain.pem Certificate + chain certs/privkey.pem Private key
  1. SANs on the cert must include PIHERDER_HOSTNAME.
  2. Permissions (recommended on the host):
chmod 600 certs/privkey.pem
chmod 644 certs/fullchain.pem
  1. Restart Caddy:
docker compose up -d caddy
  1. Browser should show a trusted lock for PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL.

Also see the repo certs/README.md.

docs.title: Outer reverse proxy (NPM, etc.)
docs.text:
You may terminate TLS at Nginx Proxy Manager (or similar) and reverse-proxy to web:8000 or to Caddy. Keep:

  • A stable public origin in PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL
  • Correct X-Forwarded-For / client IP if you use API token IP allowlists

docs.title: Local development without certs
docs.text:
# Example: use Caddyfile.dev in compose override
# (see project Caddyfile.dev — self-signed)

Push and reliable mobile install need real trusted HTTPS.

docs.title: Next
docs.text: Configure PWA & Web Push after TLS works.

docs.title: 1. Clone and enter the repo
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/bjorngluck/piherder.git
cd piherder
# optional: pin a release
# git checkout v0.4.0
cp .env.example .env

docs.title: 2. Generate the master key
docs.text:
python3 -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())"

Paste into .env:

PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY=...   # output of Fernet.generate_key()
SECRET_KEY=...            # e.g. openssl rand -hex 32

docs.title: 3. (Recommended) Set public hostname
docs.text:
# .env
PIHERDER_HOSTNAME=piherder.example.com
PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL=https://piherder.example.com:8443

Place trusted PEMs in certs/ — full steps: Trusted HTTPS & TLS.

Local only / no certs yet

Mount Caddyfile.dev instead of Caddyfile for self-signed TLS. Fine for desktop testing; not reliable for phone Web Push.

docs.title: 4. Start the stack
docs.text:
docker compose up -d --build

First build vendors frontend assets and needs outbound network (or pre-vendored static files). Schema migrations run on web startup via Alembic.

docs.title: 5. Open the UI
docs.text:
Access URL With Caddy + certs https://your.host:8443 (or your PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL) Caddy HTTP http://your.host:8888 Direct to web (no Caddy) http://localhost:8000

Continue: First login.

docs.title: Verify containers
docs.text:
docker compose ps
docker compose logs -f web --tail=100

Healthy web should accept HTTP; logs may show Web Push VAPID ready after first start.

docs.title: Volumes created on the host
docs.text:
Host path Purpose ./backups (or PIHERDER_BACKUP_HOST_PATH) Per-server rsync destinations ./piherder_backups PiHerder self-backup archives ./piherder_data Avatars / logos ./certs TLS PEMs for Caddy

Details: Volumes.

docs.title: Optional: published image
docs.text: When multi-arch images are published (target v0.5.0 RC), you can replace build: . with an image tag. Until then, compose build is the primary path. See Publish image.

docs.title: Upgrade later
docs.text:
git fetch --tags
git checkout v0.4.0   # or newer tag
docker compose up -d --build

Always keep a self-backup and the same PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY before major upgrades.

docs.title: PiHerder host (where the stack runs)
docs.text: Need Notes Linux host with Docker + Docker Compose v2 x86_64 or arm64 (Pi 4/5 OK for small fleets) Disk for images + volumes Postgres, backups destination, herder archives, avatars Ports Default compose: 8888 (HTTP), 8443 (HTTPS), optional direct 8000 to web Network to fleet SSH (22 or custom) to managed hosts

docs.title: Resource ballpark
docs.text:
Fleet size Rough guide Lab / few Pis 2 CPU, 2–4 GB RAM Larger fleet Raise CELERY_CONCURRENCY; more RAM for parallel rsync

See Multi-worker Celery.

docs.title: Managed hosts (the fleet)
docs.text: OS Support notes Raspberry Pi OS / Debian / Ubuntu Full path: apt OS patch, least-priv user scripts, rsync sudo HAOS / specialised Key deploy + plain rsync guidance; no automated least-priv

docs.title: Tools by feature
docs.text:
PiHerder probes dependencies for enabled features only (no auto-install on the remote).

Feature enabled Remote tools expected Always SSH + shell Backups rsync on PATH; sudo -n rsync or plain rsync (root/HAOS) Docker / containers docker (+ group/socket as needed) OS patch apt-get / apt

docs.title: Operator workstation
docs.text:
• Modern browser (Chrome/Firefox/Safari/Edge)
  • For Web Push on phones: trusted HTTPS + (iOS) Add to Home Screen — see PWA & Web Push

docs.title: Secrets you must keep safe
docs.text:
Secret Why PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY Fernet key for SSH keys, integration tokens, template secrets, VAPID private key SECRET_KEY Session/JWT signing Postgres password If you change defaults TLS private key certs/privkey.pem

Never commit .env or PEMs

They are gitignored. Losing PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY means encrypted fields in a restored DB/self-backup cannot be decrypted.

docs.title: Grafana
docs.text:
Read-mostly deep links into an existing Grafana. PiHerder does not have to deploy Grafana (you may still use the Grafana template for a new instance).

Kinds + tabbed bindings. wireframe

docs.title: Connect
docs.text:
1. Grafana → Administration → Service accounts — Viewer token (glsa_…) recommended.
  2. Probe:
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" "https://grafana.example.com/api/health"
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" \
  "https://grafana.example.com/api/search?type=dash-db" | head
  1. PiHerder → Integrations → + Grafana — base URL, optional token, query templates (var- prefix).
  2. Poll / Test stores health + inventory (with token).
  3. Bind with a kind:
Kind Surfaces Host metrics / Host logs Server detail rows Containers (host) Server detail Containers + container name Docker page chip / ⋯ / expand

docs.title: Placeholders
docs.text: {hostname}, {hostname_short}, {container}, {project}, {ip}, … Grafana variables need the var- prefix (var-job=…).

docs.title: Docker UX
docs.text:
• Grafana chip (tap opens filtered dashboard)
  • Container ⋯ menu
  • Expanded row links (mobile-friendly)

Without a token you can still deep-link by pasting dashboard UIDs; inventory list will be empty.

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
Optional integration hub (top-level Integrations nav — not under Settings).

Core fleet ops work without any integration.

Product Role Uptime Kuma Status, TLS days, deep links, Services pages Grafana Dashboard inventory, deep links with query templates

Credentials are Fernet-encrypted and included in self-backup.

docs.title: Design principle
docs.text:
• PiHerder owns fleet truth
  • External tools enrich via adapters and deep links
  • Prefer n8n + token REST over embedding every vendor API

docs.title: Uptime Kuma
docs.text:
You can deploy Kuma via Templates, then connect the integration for status and bindings.

Connect + bind SSH/services/Docker. wireframe

docs.title: Connect
docs.text:
1. In Kuma: Settings → API Keys — create a key (copy once).
  2. From a host that can reach Kuma:
curl -sS -u ":$KUMA_API_KEY" "https://uptime.example.com/metrics" | head
  1. PiHerder → Integrations → + Uptime Kuma — base URL + API key → Save.
  2. Optional (recommended on Kuma 1.23): username/password for dashboard ID map (/dashboard/{id}). Metrics often omit monitor_id.
  3. Poll interval default 60s; Test / Poll now.

docs.title: Binding scopes
docs.text:
Scope Where you see it SSH reachability Server chips, detail, Services Host service (no Docker) Server detail, Services — e.g. HAOS Docker project/container Docker chips + Services
  • Suggest matches maps unbound servers to TCP/SSH monitors.
  • HTTP monitors expose TLS valid + days remaining.
  • Down transitions → in-app notifications (+ optional Web Push: Integration monitor down).

docs.title: Services UI
docs.text: Path Purpose /integrations Connect + bind /servers/{id}/services Per-host services /services Fleet icon grid Dashboard Services count tile

docs.title: Logos
docs.text: Auto favicon fetch + manual upload under DATA_ROOT/service_logos/ (./piherder_data).

docs.title: API tokens (/api/v1)
docs.text:
Automation API for n8n, Home Assistant, scripts. Browser UI uses session cookies separately.

Where: Settings → API management (/herder-backups?tab=api) — admin only. Interactive OpenAPI: on your instance at /docs (tag api-v1). Long reference: docs/API.md in the repo.

docs.title: Auth
docs.text:
Authorization: Bearer ph_<secret>
  • Secret shown once at create or rotate; only a hash is stored.
  • Rotate issues a new secret immediately.
  • Revoke soft-disables; row kept for audit.

docs.title: Scopes
docs.text: Scope Allows read Catalog, health, servers, jobs GET jobs POST …/servers/{id}/jobs edit PATCH …/features feature:backup / os / docker Optional allowlist; if any set, only those features

docs.title: IP allowlist
docs.text: Optional IPs/CIDRs per token. Enforced using Caddy-forwarded client IP — call via ports 8888/8443.

docs.title: CORS
docs.text: Off by default. Server-side n8n/HA/curl do not need CORS. Set CORS_ORIGINS only for browser apps on other origins (exact origins; never *).

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
export PH_TOKEN='ph_…'
export PH_URL='https://piherder.example.com'

curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $PH_TOKEN" "$PH_URL/api/v1" | jq .
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $PH_TOKEN" "$PH_URL/api/v1/servers" | jq .

curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $PH_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"job_type":"backup"}' \
  "$PH_URL/api/v1/servers/1/jobs"

Prefer least privilege: e.g. n8n backup token = read + jobs + feature:backup + n8n host IP.

docs.title: Environment reference
docs.text:
Full commented catalog: .env.example in the repo. Copy to .env.

Compose injects matching keys into web and celery-worker. Caddy mainly needs PIHERDER_HOSTNAME.

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
Variable Purpose PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY Fernet key — SSH keys, integration tokens, template secrets, VAPID private SECRET_KEY Session / JWT signing — long random in production

Generate master key:

python3 -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())"

docs.title: Public URL / TLS
docs.text: Variable Purpose PIHERDER_HOSTNAME Caddy site hostname; must match cert SANs PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL Canonical origin (include :8443 if mapped)

docs.title: Host paths
docs.text:
Variable Default Purpose PIHERDER_BACKUP_HOST_PATH ./backups Host side of /backups mount

Other mounts fixed in docker-compose.yml: piherder_backups, piherder_data, certs.

docs.title: Database / Redis
docs.text: Variable Default idea DATABASE_URL postgresql://piherder:piherder@db:5432/piherder CELERY_BROKER_URL redis://redis:6379/0 CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND same Redis CELERY_CONCURRENCY 2 pool slots PIHERDER_SERVER_LOCK_TTL 7200 backup mutex TTL

docs.title: Auth / metrics / CORS
docs.text: Variable Purpose METRICS_TOKEN Bearer for GET /metrics METRICS_BACKUP_STALE_HOURS Stale backup gauge (default 36) CORS_ORIGINS Exact browser origins for /api/v1 (empty = off) WEBHOOK_URL / WEBHOOK_NUMBER Legacy webhook → e.g. Signal via n8n VAPID_* Optional pin; auto-gen is default PIHOLE_URL Dashboard link

docs.title: Inside-container paths (rarely change)
docs.text: BACKUP_ROOT, HERDER_BACKUP_ROOT, DATA_ROOT, DEFAULT_DOCKER_BASE, …

docs.title: Prometheus (GET /metrics)
docs.text:
Scrape-time gauges (DB only, no SSH). Not behind login cookies.

Env Purpose METRICS_TOKEN If set, require Authorization: Bearer or X-Metrics-Token METRICS_BACKUP_STALE_HOURS Hours without successful backup → stale (default 36) 
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: piherder
    metrics_path: /metrics
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["web:8000"]   # compose service name
    authorization:
      type: Bearer
      credentials: "<METRICS_TOKEN>"

Useful series: piherder_up, piherder_db_up, piherder_servers*, piherder_jobs*, piherder_notifications_open*, piherder_servers_backup_stale.

If METRICS_TOKEN is empty, treat /metrics like private-network only.

docs.title: Webhooks → Signal (or similar)
docs.text:
WEBHOOK_URL=https://your-n8n-or-bridge/...
WEBHOOK_NUMBER=+1...
# WEBHOOK_RECIPIENTS=["+1..."]

Typical pattern: PiHerder → n8n webhook → Signal CLI. In-app notifications and Web Push work without webhooks.

docs.title: Multi-worker Celery
docs.text:
Backups run in parallel across different hosts. The same host never has two active backups (Redis mutex piherder:server_lock:backup:{server_id}).

Concept Meaning Node One Celery worker process/container Pool slots Prefork children (CELERY_CONCURRENCY)

Default: 1 node · 2 pool slots. Prefer raising CELERY_CONCURRENCY before scaling containers.

Knob Default Notes CELERY_CONCURRENCY 2 Pool slots PIHERDER_SERVER_LOCK_TTL 7200 Lock TTL if worker dies mid-rsync Shared volumes required Same /backups on web + worker Cancel Revoke via celery_task_id; mutex released in finally

Optional multi-container:

# remove fixed container_name from celery-worker in compose, then:
docker compose up -d --scale celery-worker=N

Status shows N nodes and sum of pool slots.

docs.title: Self-backup & DR
docs.text:
Settings → PiHerder backup — not the same as per-server rsync backups.

Archives: format v2 .tar.gz under ./piherder_backups → /herder_backups. Host dir must be writable by container user (uid 1000).

docs.title: Included
docs.text: Content Notes Servers Encrypted SSH keys/passwords, schedules, inventory, flags Users Hashes, roles, profile, encrypted TOTP TOTP backup codes + trusted devices 2FA recovery state Docker compose versions Multi-file history Push VAPID + subscriptions Same master key on restore Notifications Recent (capped) Integrations + bindings Encrypted credentials Operational settings Timezone, force 2FA, schedules (appsetting) Avatars Packed under data/avatars/… Templates + stack deployments Ciphertext secrets Audit log Full mode only (optional, capped)

docs.title: Not included
docs.text: Content Why Jobs queue Ephemeral Per-server rsync files Different volume Service logo files Re-fetch/upload after DR (v0.5 stretch B08) External Kuma/Grafana instances Only PiHerder-side config

docs.title: Restore
docs.text:
1. Dry-run previews counts.
  2. Apply upserts by id/email/endpoint.
  3. Encrypted fields need the same PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY.
  4. Restart web so scheduler / VAPID pick up DB state.

docs.title: Schedule
docs.text: Enable cron + mode (config_only | full) + keep count in Settings. Manual run available.

docs.title: Stack Status
docs.text:
Where: Settings → Status (admin).

Component health + mount free. wireframe

docs.title: What is checked
docs.text:
Component Idea Web Process / health PostgreSQL SELECT 1 Redis Broker ping Celery Nodes + pool slots (CELERY_CONCURRENCY) APScheduler Running + jobs registered Disk (fast) Mount free on /backups, /data, /herder_backups Disk (lazy) Full tree du + host folders — View details only
  • Manual Check now + ~2 minute scheduled poll.
  • Notifications on state transition only (no spam).
  • Metrics gauges can reflect last check.

docs.title: Host dependency check (fleet)
docs.text: Separate from stack Status: per-server remote tools for enabled features. Server detail → Re-check · also after SSH test / key deploy / least-priv.

docs.title: Upgrades
docs.text:
# Config DR first
# Settings → PiHerder backup → run now
# Also snapshot Postgres volume if you can

git fetch --tags
git checkout v0.4.0   # or newer
docker compose up -d --build
# Alembic runs on web startup
docker compose run --rm --no-deps web pytest -q   # optional

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• Self-backup successful
  • PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY unchanged and backed up offline
  • Read release notes under docs/RELEASE_v*.md
  • docker compose ps healthy
  • Smoke: login, one server, optional template

docs.title: Breaking notes
docs.text: Read the release doc for the version you jump to (migrations, new env keys, behaviour changes).

docs.title: Volumes
docs.text:
Host path Container Purpose ${PIHERDER_BACKUP_HOST_PATH:-./backups} /backups rsync destinations for server backups ./piherder_backups /herder_backups PiHerder self-backup archives (chown uid 1000 if permission errors) ./piherder_data /data Avatars + service logos (Settings live in Postgres) ./certs /certs (Caddy, ro) fullchain.pem + privkey.pem

Secondary disk example:

PIHERDER_BACKUP_HOST_PATH=/home/you/backup

web and celery-worker must share the same /backups (and usually /data, /herder_backups) for multi-worker correctness.

docs.title: Deploy a template
docs.text: Wizard: variables → host → preview → confirm. wireframe

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
1. Templates → open a template → Deploy… (or Details → Deploy).
  2. Fill variables (incl. volume mode for storage vars; generate secrets if offered).
  3. Pick a Docker-enabled host (inventory counts shown).
  4. Preview rendered files (secrets masked).
  5. Confirm deploy — blocking wait modal while PiHerder:
  6. writes files over SSH
  7. locks host .env (chmod 600)
  8. runs compose pull + up -d
  9. Desired state Vn stored encrypted in PiHerder.
  10. Post-deploy checklist (manual DNS, first login, …).

docs.title: Redeploy
docs.text: From the deployment page for that host+project — same wait modal. (Volume editor polish is planned for v0.5.0.)

docs.title: Create / edit a template
docs.text:
1. Templates → + New template or From host… or Edit.
  2. Metadata: slug, name, category, version.
  3. Paste or pull docker-compose.yml; use {{VAR}} placeholders.
  4. Variables as form rows. Types include boolean + volume.
  5. Editor tools:
  6. Scan vars + volumes
  7. Move secrets → .env
  8. Checklist rows for operators.
  9. Save → source=user.

docs.title: Import zip
docs.text: Archive with template.yaml + files/. Fully editable after import.

docs.title: Security settings
docs.text:
Settings → Security policy:

Option Effect Require 2FA for all users Force 2FA for the whole UI Require 2FA for template deploy & secrets Operator must have TOTP to confirm deploy or view/edit secrets

Step-up unlock for cleartext secrets: Secrets model.

docs.title: From host
docs.text: Turn an existing compose project into an operator-owned template.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Templates → From host…
  2. Pick a Docker-enabled server + project.
  3. Optional: move secret-like values to .env placeholders.
  4. Pull parameterizes volumes, host ports, booleans, and env/secrets into deploy variables; rewrites short mounts/ports to {{VAR}}.
  5. Review in the editor → Save.
  6. Progress overlay / wait modal while SSH pull runs.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Multi-file and odd layouts: polish continues in v0.5.0 — if pull fails, see Templates troubleshooting.
  • After save, you can deploy to the same or another host.

docs.title: Service templates
docs.text:
Templates (top-level nav) are your versioned stack definitions. Create, edit, and save them; deploy is separate.

Shipped foundation: v0.4.0 · polish/ops → v0.5.0 plan

Catalog with OOTB packs and user templates. wireframe

docs.title: OOTB pack
docs.text:
Slug Service npm Nginx Proxy Manager uptime-kuma Uptime Kuma pihole Pi-hole grafana Grafana

Defaults use named volumes; deploy can switch to project folder or host path.

docs.title: Operator ownership
docs.text: Source Behaviour builtin Seeded from disk service_templates/; refreshed if still builtin and checksum changes user After Save in UI — never auto-overwritten by disk import Zip upload → editable as user

docs.title: Variable types
docs.text:
Type Deploy UI Notes string / port / int / url / email Normal fields Ports 1–65535 password Secret field Optional auto-generate boolean Yes / No true_value / false_value volume Storage mode + name/path named · ./ project · absolute host path

Volume and boolean vars are never secrets (no step-up 2FA).

docs.title: Next
docs.text:
• Deploy a template
  • From host
  • Secrets model
  • Developer schema

docs.title: Template secrets model
docs.text:
Home-production stance (locked decision):

Layer Behaviour PiHerder Source of truth; secrets Fernet-encrypted; edit / audit / redeploy here UI reveal Cleartext only after View secrets with step-up TOTP (even if you already 2FA’d at login). Unlock cookie ~10 minutes; Hide secrets clears it Host project Locked-down .env (chmod 600) for Compose ${VAR}; offline restarts work without PiHerder Docker page Template-managed stacks show a Template badge; full compose editor gated Not default Compose ./secrets/ files, Swarm secrets, vault inject — future / advanced

Master key

Restore of encrypted template / deployment secrets requires the same PIHERDER_MASTER_KEY.

docs.title: Self-backup
docs.text: Herder self-backup includes template catalog rows and stack deployments (ciphertext only).

docs.title: Advanced stores
docs.text: Swarm / vault / sealed host blobs remain post-0.5 / Horizon 3 exploration — not the default path.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Page SSH / key / deps / docker group SSH, rsync & dependencies Backup failed / stuck pending Backups No push on phone / PWA Push / PWA Template deploy / Docker editor Templates & Docker

docs.title: Always useful
docs.text:
docker compose ps
docker compose logs web --tail=200
docker compose logs celery-worker --tail=200
  • Jobs page for work unit status + log tail
  • Audit for who/what
  • Settings → Status for stack health
  • Server Re-check dependencies for remote tools

docs.title: Failed status
docs.text:
• Open job detail + audit for rsync output.
  • Per-source rc != 0 → overall failed; last_backup_at unchanged.
  • Path not allowed by policy?
  • Disk full on PiHerder /backups mount? Status → View details.

docs.title: Stuck pending / waiting_for_server
docs.text:
• Another backup holds the Redis per-server mutex.
  • Wait or cancel the active job.
  • Worker crash: lock TTL eventually expires (PIHERDER_SERVER_LOCK_TTL).
  • Celery down? Settings → Status.

docs.title: Celery not running jobs
docs.text:
docker compose ps celery-worker
docker compose logs celery-worker --tail=100

Confirm CELERY_BROKER_URL and shared volumes.

docs.title: Permission on herder_backups volume
docs.text:
Self-backup (not server backup) needs uid 1000 write on ./piherder_backups:

sudo chown -R 1000:1000 piherder_backups

docs.title: No push on Android
docs.text:
1. Trusted HTTPS (not self-signed).
  2. PIHERDER_PUBLIC_URL matches the origin you open.
  3. Web logs show VAPID ready.
  4. Account → Enable on this device → Send test.
  5. OS/browser notification permission granted.

docs.title: No push on iOS
docs.text:
1. iOS 16.4+.
  2. Add to Home Screen and open from the icon.
  3. Plain Safari tab will not work.
  4. Trusted cert required.

docs.title: Push used to work, then died
docs.text:
• VAPID keys rotated / DB wiped without same keys → re-enable on each device.
  • Browser endpoint expired → Enable again.
  • Subscription still listed but stale — remove and re-add.

docs.title: In-app notifications work, push does not
docs.text: Expected if VAPID missing or insecure origin — fix TLS/VAPID first.

docs.title: Cannot connect
docs.text:
1. Hostname/IP and port correct?
  2. Firewall allows SSH from PiHerder host?
  3. Test connection on SSH access panel.
  4. Key deployed? Password only for bootstrap.

docs.title: Key deploy fails
docs.text:
• Password session required if key not yet installed.
  • authorized_keys permissions on remote (~/.ssh 700, file 600).
  • SELinux/AppArmor rare edge cases on some distros.

docs.title: Backups: permission denied / rsync
docs.text:
• Non-root: need sudo -n rsync (passwordless) for protected paths.
  • Root/HAOS: plain rsync path auto-detected.
  • Path policy may deny the source — check allow/deny on Backups page.

docs.title: Docker commands fail after least-priv
docs.text:
• User in docker group? re-login / new session.
  • Docker base dir absolute path + Option B ACL if stacks under another home.
  • ~/docker expands to wrong home after username switch.

docs.title: Dependency chips red
docs.text: Re-check dependencies — install missing packages on the host yourself (PiHerder does not auto-install).

docs.title: Wait modal sits forever
docs.text:
• SSH hang on host (disk, pull, network).
  • Check Jobs/Audit if available; docker compose logs web.
  • Host docker compose interactive prompts?

docs.title: Deploy “succeeded” but stack unhealthy
docs.text: v0.4.0+ Deploy surfaces pull/up codes — re-read banner/audit. SSH to host and docker compose ps in the project dir.

docs.title: Cannot edit compose on a template stack
docs.text: By design — use deployment desired state / redeploy. See Secrets & template badge.

docs.title: From-host pull incomplete
docs.text: Odd multi-file layouts still hardening in v0.5.0. Manually create template and paste compose if needed.

docs.title: Step-up 2FA for secrets fails
docs.text:
• User must have TOTP enabled.
  • “Require 2FA for template deploy” setting on?
  • Unlock cookie expired (~10 min) — View secrets again.

docs.title: Inventory stale
docs.text: Force refresh on Docker page — Inventory cache.
